commit.message:
Fix 36 audit findings: bugs, security, error handling

Bugs fixed (11):
- Critics (Scored, Factual, Binary) now fail-safe on exceptions instead of passing
- QualityGate fails-safe on LLM evaluation failure
- Escalation max_total_attempts properly enforced
- build_loop: pathlib for cross-platform paths, fix always-true condition
- Code generation: fix triple-quote escaping, fix dead builtin path
- orchestrator: initialize _tool_start before try block
- archetypes: case-insensitive agent name matching

Security fixed (10):
- SSRF: browser_tool and webhook_tool import validation at top level (no silent skip)
- Pipe injection: check ALL segments, not just last
- Git args: shlex.quote() on all user input, not just commit messages
- File tool: symlink traversal prevention (check before and after resolve)
- file_tool + sql_tool: closure-based state instead of mutable globals
- Dashboard: XSS prevention via json.dumps() on API_BASE
- API key logging truncated to last 8 chars

Error handling fixed (15):
- domain_analyzer: 6 fallback paths now log warnings
- agent.py: guard empty conversation, log invalid JSON args
- planner.py: JSON parse fallback to single-step plan
- observability: null-check on DB connection
- human.py: webhook poll exceptions logged
- inbound.py: case-insensitive file watching on Windows

All 1,008 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(conductor-qa): redirect gemini output to file only; fix color switch

- Tee-Object was writing gemini stdout to the function pipeline, making
  $result a multi-element array; switch produced one color per element,
  crashing Write-Host -ForegroundColor with a concatenated string.
- Use Out-File instead so Invoke-QA returns only the status string.
- Resolve color into a variable before passing to Write-QA.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: update PROGRESS.md — Fase 3 & 4 selesai (~44%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Codespaces. Since this runs as a run_once_after script, dotfiles
are already applied; tool installation can be retried manually.

commit.message:
feat(worktree): clean -B で orphan な nas/* ブランチも削echoecho

worktree が削除済みだがブランチだけ残っている orphan ブランチを
listOrphanNasBranches() で検出し、-B フラグ指定時にまとめて削除する。

- listOrphanNasBranches() を追加: git branch --list nas/* から
  worktree に紐づかないブランチを抽出
- cleanNasWorktrees() の戻り値を CleanResult に変更
  (worktrees, orphanBranches の件数をecho)
- CLI 側の出力を CleanResult に合わせて更新
- git worktree prune を事前実行して stale 参照をクリーンアップ

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: apply OOP design principles to core design (#8)

* Initial plan

* refactor: apply OOP design principles to core design

- SRP: decompose Castle.create() into _ensure_dirs(), _build_skill_manager(),
  and _build_channels() private helpers — each with a single responsibility
- DRY + Delegation: extract Session._assemble() classmethod shared by
  Session.create() and Session.resume(), eliminating duplicated setup code
- SRP + Delegation: extract _record_turn_end() helper from agent_loop() so
  trace recording is isolated from hook dispatch and loop orchestration

feat(backend): implement Fase 4 - Modul Jadwal & Progress

- MilestoneRepository: CRUD + GetWeightedProgress (SQL weighted-avg) + GetCurrentPlannedProgress
- ProgressRepository: CRUD ProgressUpdate + FotoProgress (soft delete)
- KurvaSRepository: KurvaSPoint struct + GetKurvaS (iterasi per minggu, plan & aktual kumulatif)
- JadwalService: CRUD milestone + computeStatus (selesai/terlambat/sedang_berjalan/belum_mulai)
- ProgressService: CreateProgress replace actual_persen + recalc status + simpan foto
- JadwalHandler: 10 endpoint (milestone CRUD, progress CRUD, kurva-s, progress-summary)
- Router: +10 routes dengan RBAC (owner+manajer CRUD; semua bisa baca & tambah progress)

Verifikasi:
  go build ./...  → 0 error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: fix security vulnerabilities and bump Backstage to 1.48.4 (#741)

* chore: fix security vulnerabilities and bump Backstage to 1.48.4

- Backstage 1.48.1 → 1.48.4 (fixes @backstage/plugin-scaffolder-backend, @backstage/integration, @backstage/plugin-techdocs-node vulns)
- koa 2.16.1 → 2.16.4 (Host Header Injection)
- fast-xml-parser → 5.3.8 (stack overflow)
- basic-ftp → 5.2.0 (Path Traversal - critical)
- multer → 2.1.1 (DoS vulnerabilities)
- svgo → 2.8.1 (DoS via entity expansion)
- underscore → 1.13.8 (Prototype Pollution)
- rollup → 4.59.0 (Arbitrary File Write via Path Traversal)
- dompurify → 3.3.2 (XSS vulnerabilities)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: fix additional security vulnerabilities

- @tootallnate/once → 3.0.1 (Incorrect Control Flow Scoping)
- immutable → 4.3.8 (Prototype Pollution - high)
- undici → 6.23.0 (unbounded decompression chain - medium)
- jsonpath resolution → ^1.2.0 (fixes Prototype Pollution alert #165 - medium)
- @azure/msal-node in security-metrics-backend: 3.8.5 → 5.0.6

Note: ajv (v6/v8 split incompatibility), minimatch (multiple major version ranges),
elliptic (no patch), @octokit/* (breaking major bumps), jsonpath #171 (no patch
available) cannot be resolved without breaking changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: eliminate SSR HTML generation — VNext renders everything (#1370)

* refactor: eliminate SSR HTML generation — VNext renders everything (#1369)

Remove 5,700+ lines of server-side HTML generation that duplicated
what the VNext SPA already renders client-side.

DataScaffold.cs (1,271 lines removed):
- BuildFormFields, BuildViewRowsHtml, BuildTreeViewHtml,
  BuildOrgChartHtml, BuildTimetableHtml and all private HTML helpers
- Dead methods: CoerceTimeOnly, TryBuildLookupUrl, ToInputString,
  CountByType, IsHighCardinalityLookup, GetLookupDisplayValue,
  GetCreateDefaultInputString, RenderChildListEditorHtml
- BuildListRows boolean/lookup output simplified to plain text
  (export path strips HTML anyway)

RouteHandlers.cs (1,046 lines removed):
- Dead SSR: BuildViewSwitcher, BuildTimelineViewHtml, GetViewTypeName,
  BuildPageSizeSelector, BuildEnhancedPagination, BuildUrlWithParam,
  BuildSortableColumnHeaders, BuildTableWithSortableHeaders,
  BuildBulkActionsBar, BuildCommandButtonsHtml, BuildFormFieldsWithErrors,
  BuildExportDropdown
- Dead code: ReturnObjectDictionary, ReturnQueryClauseList,
  ExportHierarchicalJson, ExportFlatCsv, ExportMultiSheetZip,
  BuildHtmlTableDocument, BuildRtfDocument, ApplyUserPasswordForImport,
  ApplyPrefillFromQuery, AppendUserPasswordFieldsIfNeeded,
  ApplyUserPasswordIfNeeded, IsEntityAccessible (duplicate of OpenApiHandler)

Tests (3,422 lines removed):
- Deleted 7 pure SSR test files
- Removed SSR test methods from 4 mixed test files

What remains server-side:
- VNext shell/chrome (ServeVNextShell)
- Schema/metadata API endpoints (BuildEntitySchema)
- Data API (JSON CRUD)
- Export: CSV, list rows (BuildListRows, BuildListHeaders)
- Setup wizard, error pages

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: remove 8 dead methods (108 lines)

Remove unused private/internal methods found via dead code scan:

DataScaffold.cs:
- LoadByIdForType (orphaned helper)
- MapChildInputType (SSR remnant)
- EscapeJs (SSR remnant)

RouteHandlers.cs:
- ViewRowsToArray, PrependIdRow, ConcatArrays, BuildParentValueRow (dead array utils)
- EscapeRtf (unused RTF export helper)

All had exactly 1 occurrence (definition only, zero callers).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- IRouteHandlers: removed 4 SSR interface members

Test cleanup:

Support word boundary assertions in bitnfa engine (#25)

* Initial plan

* Support word boundary assertions (\b / \B) in bitnfa engine

Co-authored-by: strimo378 <59825937+strimo378@users.noreply.github.com>

* Support word boundary (\b / \B) assertions in bitnfa engine

Add _reduce_to_position_nfa_boundary() which doubles NFA positions into
non-word/word variants and evaluates boundary epsilons via conditional
epsilon closure during transition-mask construction.

Key changes:
- compiler.py: new _reduce_to_position_nfa_boundary() function; compile_nfa()
  now routes to it when has_boundary is set instead of raising ValueError
- test_word_boundary.py: parametrize tests over both dfa and bitnfa engines;
  remove test_bitnfa_rejects_boundary (no longer applicable)
- Golden files: add word_boundary_bitnfa.c and non_word_boundary_bitnfa.c
- test_golden.py / update_golden.py: register new golden entries

Co-authored-by: strimo378 <59825937+strimo378@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update README: link bitnfa golden file for word boundary feature

Co-authored-by: strimo378 <59825937+strimo378@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace hadolint Docker action with direct binary

The hadolint-action@v3 runs hadolint inside a Docker container with
failure-threshold: info (default), which fails silently without
showing actual lint output. Switch to direct binary installation
(v2.12.0) which respects .hadolint.yaml config reliably and shows
clear output on failure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(worktree): avoid branch -f when target is checked out

When the target branch (e.g. main) is checked out in a worktree,
cherry-pick directly in that worktree instead of using 'branch -f'
which would desync the working tree. Only use the detached HEAD +
branch -f approach when the branch isn't checked out anywhere.

Also refuse cherry-pick if the target worktree has uncommitted changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(worktree): use detached HEAD for cherry-pick temp worktree

git worktree add fails with 'already used by worktree' when the
target branch (e.g. main) is checked out elsewhere. Fix by creating
the temp worktree with --detach at the target commit, then advancing
the branch ref with 'git branch -f' after cherry-pick succeeds.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: pass config path to hadolint-action explicitly

The action runs hadolint inside a Docker container where automatic
config discovery may not find .hadolint.yaml. Explicitly set
config: .hadolint.yaml for both Dockerfile lint steps.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix progress spinner visibility after agent prompt

The progress spinner uses carriage return to overwrite its line on
stderr. When the agent selection prompt writes multi-line output between
spinner calls, subsequent updates land on invisible already-scrolled
lines, giving the user no feedback.

Fix by finalizing the spinner line (progress.Done with newline) before
any interactive prompt, then restarting it (progress.Start) after the
prompt returns. Applied to get.go, install.go global path, and
update.go global path. The project branch in update.go gets its own
progress.Update call since the shared one was moved into the global
branch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: modular architecture + OOXML pipeline improvements

- Refactor monolithic synthesize.mjs (5300 lines) into 36 focused modules in lib/
  - lib/config/: config, pricing
  - lib/llm-clients/: 8 LLM provider clients (Copilot, Gemini, OpenRouter, etc.)
  - lib/loaders/: OCR, OOXML, cache loaders
  - lib/merge/: chunk optimizer, merge synthesis, super-chunk processor
  - lib/processing/: page processor, batch processor, vision retry, fallback
  - lib/prompts/: page prompts, merge prompts, system instruction
  - lib/tables/: reconciler, validation, enrichment, OCR correction
  - lib/validation/: evidence validator
  - lib/utils/: cache, helpers, logger
  - lib/stats/: run stats
  - lib/output/: synthesis saver

- Add extract-ooxml.mjs: parse .xlsx ZIP for shapes, connectors, pictures, cells

- OOXML cache invalidation: re-process pages when OOXML shapes are available
  but cached summary was generated without them (hadOoxml flag)

- Callout → cell mapping: resolve shape positions to actual cell values in prompt

- Quality improvements: table dedup, evidence normalization, merge prompt rules

- Bump CACHE_VERSION to 1.2.0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: AI Workflow Analysis Agent with remediation knowledge base

New components:
- src/lib/agent/remediation-kb.ts: Per-tool knowledge base with:
  - 13 finding patterns across identity, defender, posture, device, data protection, infra
  - Proven Azure CLI + PowerShell remediation scripts per pattern
  - Microsoft Learn doc links for every remediation item
  - Verification commands to confirm fixes worked

- src/lib/agent/workflow-analyzer.ts: Agentic analysis agent that:
  - Receives all workflow step results as context
  - Injects remediation KB into system prompt (tool-specific scripts + docs)
  - Can make up to 3 additional MCP tool calls for deeper investigation
  - Produces: Executive Verdict, Cross-Step Correlations, Risk Score (0-100),
    Remediation Plan with real scripts + doc links, Recommended Next Workflows
  - Returns toolCallsMade[] showing what additional investigation was done

UI integration:
- After all workflow steps complete, shows '🧠 Analyze with AI' button
- Analysis rendered as rich markdown with prose styling
- Export includes analysis in the report (Copy + Download)
- Re-analyze button to regenerate with fresh LLM call

Tests (29/29 passing):
- New test: 'AI Workflow Analysis' runs privileged-access-review workflow,
  executes 3 steps, calls analyze API, verifies analysis contains:
  verdict ✅, risk score ✅, remediation scripts ✅, real az commands ✅
- Verified against live tenant: found 12 excessive role findings,
  AI produced 3425-char analysis with specific user remediation

Android:

- Generated Kotlin bindings (1620 lines) + JNA dependency

commit.message:
feat(lsp): xmldoc generation, incremental sync, diagnostics & navigation

- Switch to TextDocumentSyncKind.Incremental; build document text by
  applying per-change events so undo/paste never re-trigger doc expansion
- Add '/' to completion trigger characters; doc comment template is now
  returned as a snippet (InsertTextFormat.Snippet with tab stops) so the
  cursor lands inside <summary> after acceptance
- Detect client snippetSupport capability; fall back to plain-text template
  when the client does not support snippets
- Guard completion: no regular completions on '///'-only lines; no template
  when a <summary> already exists in the doc block above the function
- CGS014 warning: flag empty type="" attributes in XML doc comments
- References, rename and highlights are now xmldoc-aware: cursor on
  name="param" in a doc comment resolves to code references and vice-versa;
  all name="param" occurrences in the doc block are included in every result
- Update README Editor Support section with plain-language feature descriptions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(security): add Zip Slip path traversal protection in llm_download.go

Add explicit path boundary check before extracting ZIP entries.
Although filepath.Base() already strips directory components, the
explicit strings.HasPrefix check satisfies CodeQL's ZipSlip query
and provides defense-in-depth against path traversal attacks.

Fixes CodeQL alert #3.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: improve robustness of tool installation scripts

- Docker GPG: add --batch --yes for idempotent re-runs
- Copilot CLI: fail gracefully (non-essential tool)
- draw.io: isolate in subshell so GitHub API failures
  don't abort the entire script

Principle: essential tools (Azure CLI, Docker, Rust) fail loudly;
optional tools (Go tools, Copilot CLI, draw.io, macOS brew)
fail with a warning and continue.

fix: handle empty cherry-pick and preserve branch on failure

- Detect already-applied commits during cherry-pick (empty working
  tree + CHERRY_PICK_HEAD present) and auto-skip them with
  git cherry-pick --skip in a loop
- Return success/failure from cherryPickToBase() so teardown can
  decide whether to delete the branch
- On cherry-pick failure, preserve the branch for manual resolution
  instead of silently deleting it

commit.message:
Add Accessibility Audit agent prompt using ACCESS protocol

Adds a comprehensive accessibility audit agent prompt covering:
- ACCESS protocol (Audit, Catalog, Classify, Evaluate, Solve, Standardize)
- WCAG 2.2 compliance (A/AA/AAA levels)
- Screen reader testing (VoiceOver, NVDA, JAWS)
- Keyboard navigation and focus management
- Color contrast and visual accessibility
- ARIA roles, states, and properties
- Semantic HTML best practices
- Cognitive and mobile/touch accessibility
- Automated testing (axe, Lighthouse, pa11y)
- Manual testing checklists and severity classification (P0-P3)
- Structured audit report template with APEI cycle methodology

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Collapsible step results — don't expand by default

Step results during workflow run are now collapsed behind a
'▶ View results' toggle. Shows only the summary line inline
(e.g., '7 alerts found'). Click to expand the full rendered
tables/badges. Results Summary section also uses collapsible
panes per step with summary + tool name visible in the header.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add deduplication and per-conflict Slack notifications

- Created deduplication module with fingerprint-based tracking
  - 42-day expiry for conflict fingerprints
  - Categorizes conflicts as new/changed/unchanged/resolved
  - State stored in .pr-conflict-state.json (gitignored)

- Rewrote slack_notify to send one message per conflict/cluster
  - Uses @mentions (assumes GitHub username = Slack username)
  - Simple 2-PR pairs show details with line ranges
  - Multi-PR clusters show cluster summary
  - Only sends for new + changed conflicts (not unchanged)

- Updated pr_conflict_detector.py to integrate deduplication
  - Loads state, compares conflicts, saves updated state
  - Filters Slack notifications to only new/changed

- Added comprehensive tests
  - test_deduplication.py with 19 tests
  - Updated test_slack_notify.py for new message format
  - Fixed test_pr_conflict_detector.py to mock deduplication

Test/lint status: 177 tests passing, 99% coverage, 9.99/10 pylint

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: pr-conflict-detector
repository.description: A GitHub Action to detect potential merge conflicts between open pull requests

commit.message:
fix(tests): resolve integration test env var collision and anyio cancel scope errors

- test_auth_flow.py: use os.environ.setdefault() instead of direct
  assignment so it does not overwrite ADMIN_USERNAME/ADMIN_PASSWORD
  already set by the test runner (caused 401s in integration tests
  when pytest collected all test modules at startup)
- test_mcp_smoke.py: inline sse_client + ClientSession setup inside
  each test function instead of using a shared async fixture; this
  keeps the anyio cancel scope in the same event loop task as the
  test body, eliminating 'Attempted to exit cancel scope in a
  different task' RuntimeErrors during fixture teardown

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix agent prompt: retry on invalid input, resolve target before manifest write

promptAgentChoice now retries up to 3 times on invalid input instead of
failing immediately. This prevents transient empty-input issues from
aborting the install flow.

Move resolveInstallTargets() (which may prompt for agent choice) before
manifest/pinfile writes in both get.go and install.go. Previously, a
failed prompt left dependencies persisted in the manifest, causing
confusing retry behavior where the user's answer was parsed as a URL
argument.

Add 6 tests for promptAgentChoice covering: valid input, Both selection,
retry on empty, retry on garbage, exhausted retries, and EOF.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(client): move changie fragments to correct unreleased directory

Changie multi-project configs expect all fragments in the shared
.changes/unreleased/ directory with a 'project' field inside the YAML
to identify which project they belong to. The fragments were incorrectly
placed in per-project subdirectories (.changes/{project}/unreleased/)
which changie does not search for unreleased changes.

Also adds required 'project' and 'time' fields to the fragments.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(vs): capture daemon stderr, add eval watchdog, improve error reporting

DaemonManager: redirect stderr (UseShellExecute=false) so startup
failures include daemon output. Added readStderr() helper.

LiveTestingSubscriber: added ConnectionLost/ConnectionRestored events
that fire when the SSE stream drops or recovers.

EvalService: detects connection loss during in-flight eval and returns
a descriptive error instead of silently failing.

InfoBarService: added ShowDaemonStartupFailedAsync (with stderr) and
ShowEvalInterruptedAsync notifications.

StatusBarManager/DaemonCommands: updated to use Process return type
and show stderr on startup failure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
